task_completion.description: Navigate to the L3 vertebral level in the axial view
task_completion.visual_evidence: The Slicer 2D view (Red slice) should show an axial cross-section of the abdomen. The vertebral body should be visible (kidney shaped bone in center-bottom). The view should be at the level of the psoas muscles (flanking the spine) and likely near the umbilicus.

task_completion.description: Create a segmentation mask for muscle tissue using thresholding
task_completion.visual_evidence: The 'Segment Editor' module should be visible. A segmentation mask (colored overlay) should be present. There may be evidence of thresholding settings (e.g., range -29 to 150 HU) visible in the UI.

task_completion.description: Ensure segmentation covers Psoas and Paraspinal muscles excluding bone/fat
task_completion.visual_evidence: The colored segmentation mask should specifically cover the muscle groups posterior and lateral to the vertebrae (Psoas and Erector Spinae). It should NOT cover the high-density bone (white) or low-density fat (black/dark grey) surrounding the muscles.

task_completion.description: Calculate Mean HU using Segment Statistics
task_completion.visual_evidence: The 'Segment Statistics' module (or similar quantification tool) should be open. A table should be visible showing the 'Mean' value for the muscle segment. The value is typically between 20 and 60 HU.

task_completion.description: Generate a JSON report with correct classification logic
task_completion.visual_evidence: A text editor or terminal output showing the content of 'myosteatosis_report.json'. The 'classification' field ('Normal' or 'Myosteatosis') must correctly correspond to the 'mean_hu' value and 'patient_sex' shown, based on the thresholds (Male <= 41, Female <= 33).

task_completion.description: Save the segmentation and report to the specified paths
task_completion.visual_evidence: Visual evidence of saving files (Save dialog or terminal commands) to '~/Documents/SlicerData/AMOS/'. Filenames 'muscle_segmentation.nii.gz' and 'myosteatosis_report.json' should be visible.

integrity.description: Verify the agent used the Slicer GUI for segmentation and analysis
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots must show the 3D Slicer application window with modules like 'Segment Editor' or 'Segment Statistics' active, rather than purely script execution in a terminal.

integrity.description: Verify report values are derived from the software analysis
integrity.visual_evidence: The 'mean_hu' value in the generated JSON report must match the value shown in the Slicer 'Segment Statistics' table in the screenshots.
The classification in the report must follow the specific logic: Male <= 41 HU or Female <= 33 HU is Myosteatosis. If the math doesn't add up, flag as invalid.

privileged_info_for_vlm: Standard skeletal muscle HU range is -29 to +150. Diagnostic thresholds for Myosteatosis: Male <= 41 HU, Female <= 33 HU. Normal muscle attenuation is typically 40-50 HU. L3 vertebral level is identified by the presence of the vertebral body and large psoas muscles flanking it, usually below the kidneys.
